stocks.investment_thesis: General Oceans is a fast-growing underwater technology platform (sensors, robotics, AUVs) serving Defence, Marine Infrastructure and Ocean Science, seeking a Q1 2026 Oslo Børs IPO with NOK 500m primary raise. The broker (acting as co-bookrunner) argues a pre-money equity value of NOK 4,000–5,330m — a 16–55% premium to the NOK 3,440m implied by cornerstone price cap — backed by DCF and peer multiples (2026e EV/EBIT 14.7–19.4x, compressing to 11.1–14.7x in 2028e). The defence/underwater technology secular tailwind is genuine but the revenue base is modest at NOK 1,304m 2025, and Arctic's conflict-of-interest as bookrunner warrants a valuation haircut.

key_claims.claim: Pre-money equity value NOK 3,440m (cornerstone cap NOK 21/share)
key_claims.verified_via: Euronext ITF announcement 9 March 2026 and Law360
key_claims.note: Directly confirmed in official Euronext listing announcement: DNB AM and Folketrygdfondet committed NOK 200m each at up to NOK 21/share, implying NOK 3,440m pre-money equity value.

key_claims.claim: Primary offering NOK 500m; secondary offering size TBC from Ferd and Lohrmann
key_claims.verified_via: Euronext ITF announcement 9 March 2026
key_claims.note: Confirmed in official ITF doc: 'new share issue of approximately NOK 500 million as well as facilitate a secondary offering of existing shares from the main existing shareholders, Atle Lohrmann and Ferd.'

key_claims.claim: Cornerstone investors Folketrygdfondet and DNB (NOK 200m each)
key_claims.verified_via: Euronext ITF announcement 9 March 2026
key_claims.note: Verbatim confirmed in official document.

key_claims.claim: Arctic's analyst values General Oceans at NOK 4,000–5,330m pre-money
key_claims.verified_via: Internal Arctic note; not independently verified; Arctic is co-bookrunner
key_claims.note: This valuation range (16–55% premium to cornerstone-implied value) is from Arctic's own analyst while Arctic acts as co-bookrunner — a clear conflict of interest. No independent analyst coverage available pre-IPO. The 2026e EV/EBIT of 14.7–19.4x is plausible for a defence-tech growth platform but sits at the top end vs. comparable listed peers. The 2025 revenue of NOK 1,304m and ~18% Adj. EBITA margin are confirmed in the ITF filing.

key_claims.claim: Revenue CAGR of ~26% from 2023 to 2025 (NOK 816m to NOK 1,304m)
key_claims.verified_via: Euronext ITF filing and company IR page
key_claims.note: Confirmed in official prospectus-related materials: revenue grew from NOK 816m (2023) to NOK 1,304m (2025) and Adj. EBITA grew from NOK 100m to NOK 235m over same period.

key_claims.claim: Company is a global provider of advanced underwater technology (sensors, robotics) to Defence, Marine Infrastructure, Ocean Science
key_claims.verified_via: Company website, ITF announcement, PitchBook, Ocean News & Technology
key_claims.note: Well substantiated. The group owns Nortek, Tritech, Klein, RS Aqua (Sensors) and Reach Robotics, SRS (Robotics). Defence accounts for up to ~50% of sales per pre-IPO reporting.

key_claims.claim: General Oceans established in 2021, building on Nortek founded 1996
key_claims.verified_via: Company IR and PitchBook
key_claims.note: Confirmed. Nortek founded 1991/1996 per various sources; General Oceans formed as platform in 2021.

stocks.bull_case: Defence/dual-use underwater technology is a structural growth market (AUV market CAGR ~10%+ per forecasters), General Oceans has demonstrated 26% revenue CAGR 2023–2025 with margin expansion to ~18% Adj. EBITA, strong brand portfolio (Nortek, Tritech, Klein) with sticky institutional customers including US, Norwegian and Royal Navies. Cornerstone backing from Folketrygdfondet and DNB AM provides credibility and liquidity. Arctic's DCF/peer valuation of NOK 4,000–5,330m implies meaningful upside to cornerstones. Brent at $103 and elevated defence budgets globally support end-market spending.
stocks.bear_case: Arctic is co-bookrunner with direct financial incentive to publish an aggressive valuation — the NOK 4,000–5,330m range is 16–55% above the NOK 3,440m cornerstone anchor which is itself the market price signal. 2024 EBIT was only NOK 68.2m (vs revenue NOK 957m) due to integration issues at Klein, SRS and Reach Robotics, raising execution risk. The M&A-driven platform model introduces goodwill and integration risk. Secondary offering (Ferd and Lohrmann selling) overhang. No post-IPO independent analyst coverage track record.
broker_flags: CONFLICT OF INTEREST: Arctic acts as co-bookrunner on the IPO while simultaneously publishing a research note with a valuation 16–55% above cornerstone price
Valuation range top end (NOK 5,330m) is 55% premium to market-implied NOK 3,440m — requires significant growth execution to justify
No prior analyst track record on this name — first call
Secondary offering overhang from Ferd and Lohrmann is unquantified ('TBC')
stocks.forum_sentiment: No Norwegian retail forum sentiment available pre-IPO; general market interest in defence/underwater tech is elevated given the ongoing geopolitical environment and Brent above $100.
stocks.insider_read: Founder Atle Lohrmann is subject to a 365-day lock-up per the ITF announcement; Ferd (co-owner) has a 180-day lock-up — both signals of standard insider alignment, though Lohrmann and Ferd are participating in the secondary offering indicating partial exit.
stocks.short_interest_read: Not yet listed; no short interest data available pre-IPO.
stocks.catalyst_note: IPO expected Q1 2026; prospectus publication is the next key event. PDIE management meetings being arranged by Arctic. Key post-listing catalysts: Q1 2026 results to validate the ~18% Adj. EBITA margin and defence order momentum; any new large defence contracts.
stocks.vs_consensus: No independent consensus exists — Arctic is a bookrunner and the only publishing analyst. The NOK 3,440m cornerstone price implies the institutional market's current best estimate; Arctic's NOK 4,000–5,330m is 16–55% above that market clearing level.
stocks.one_line_verdict: Genuinely attractive defence/underwater-tech growth story with verified financials and credible cornerstones, but Arctic's bookrunner conflict of interest makes the 16–55% premium valuation claim unactionable without independent verification — seek PDIE meeting and await prospectus before sizing.

stocks.investment_thesis: Vend (formerly Schibsted Marketplaces) is a Nordic digital marketplace operator in Cars, Real Estate, Jobs, and Recommerce segments, currently trading near 52-week lows at 258.2 NOK vs. a 325 NOK consensus target. The broker flags that Q1 volumes are 'slightly supportive' led by Cars Norway, and highlights that OpenAI's apparent pullback from direct-in-chat shopping checkout provides sector tailwind. The NOK 4.5bn capital return programme (from Lendo + Adevinta Spain exits) is independently verified and active.

key_claims.claim: Q1 volumes slightly supportive vs broker Q1 estimates; broker 8% ahead of consensus on adj EBITDA
key_claims.verified_via: No independent Q1 data available; next earnings 30 April 2026
key_claims.note: Broker is claiming a volume read-through from segment data. Cars Norway positive, Real Estate slightly positive, Jobs negative — directionally plausible given Norwegian macro, but the '8% ahead of consensus' for adj EBITDA cannot be independently verified pre-earnings.

key_claims.claim: OpenAI apparently scaling back direct-in-chat shopping checkout, providing sector tailwinds
key_claims.verified_via: No confirmation of a formal OpenAI policy reversal found in search results
key_claims.note: This claim was flagged as a tailwind in the prior 2026-03-14 analysis note too. The broker uses 'apparently' — no formal OpenAI announcement found confirming a checkout rollback. The general AI/agentic commerce trend (per other sources) actually continues to intensify, suggesting the claim may be overstated or premature.

key_claims.claim: NOK 4.5bn capital return from Q1 2026 exits (Lendo, Mittanbud, Adevinta Spain)
key_claims.verified_via: MarketScreener announcement and TipRanks company news
key_claims.note: Independently confirmed: Vend received ~NOK 4.5bn in Q1 2026 cash proceeds from these exits with distribution committed to shareholders.

key_claims.claim: Sector 'bombed out cheap'
key_claims.verified_via: Financial data: VEND at 258.2 NOK, 52w low 223.2, -8% YTD, ~19% below consensus target
key_claims.note: The stock trades at Fwd P/E 26.3x and EV/EBITDA 25.9x — not cheap on absolute multiples but at a meaningful discount to the 325 NOK consensus and near 52-week lows.

stocks.bull_case: NOK 4.5bn capital return (verified) represents ~17% of current market cap and is actively being distributed; trading at ~19% discount to consensus with 12 analysts covering. Cars Norway volume uptick is consistent with Norwegian housing/mobility market recovery. OpenAI checkout retreat (if confirmed) removes a key competitive overhang for marketplace revenue models. Near 52-week lows provides a potential technical floor.
stocks.bear_case: NCA dawn raid on Nettbil (Cars Norway subsidiary) for suspected illegal cooperation remains an unresolved binary regulatory risk — investigation scope and penalty quantum are unknown. Vend is outside broker's core energy/shipping/offshore competency. Last quarter EPS was -11.89 NOK vs. +1.23 NOK estimate (a massive miss), raising earnings quality concerns. Jobs Norway segment still negative. The OpenAI tailwind claim is unverified and potentially overstated.
broker_flags: OUTSIDE BROKER COMPETENCY: Vend is a digital marketplace company, not energy/shipping/offshore
NCA Nettbil investigation: binary regulatory risk unresolved and unquantified
OpenAI shopping checkout retreat claim uses 'apparently' — not independently confirmed
Prior analyses in this thread flagged a 'fabricated PE/Dorsey narrative' in a prior email — credibility flag persists
Q4 EPS miss (-11.89 NOK vs. +1.23 estimate) casts doubt on near-term earnings visibility
stocks.forum_sentiment: No specific Shareville/Hegnar/Reddit Norway sentiment retrieved in search; general market sentiment on Nordic marketplaces is cautious given NCA overhang and weak Jobs segment, offset by capital return optimism.
stocks.insider_read: Vor Capital previously exited below 5% (noted in prior analyses) — a mild negative insider signal. No new insider buying flagged in current email or search results.
stocks.short_interest_read: No specific short interest data retrieved; the stock's -8% YTD and proximity to 52-week lows suggest the market has been net sellers, but no formal short ratio data available.
stocks.catalyst_note: April 30 Q1 2026 earnings is the primary catalyst — this will either confirm broker's +8% adj. EBITDA beat vs. consensus or invalidate the volume recovery thesis. Formal buyback programme announcement from NOK 4.5bn proceeds is the second near-term catalyst. NCA investigation outcome remains the binary risk event.
stocks.vs_consensus: 12 analysts, consensus target NOK 325.25 (range 258–417), current price 258.2 — trading at the lower bound of the consensus range, approximately 19% below the mean target. Broker is 8% above consensus on Q1 adj. EBITDA.
stocks.one_line_verdict: Capital return catalyst and near-52w-low valuation are compelling, but the unresolved NCA/Nettbil binary risk, outside-competency coverage, and an unverified OpenAI tailwind claim cap conviction — maintain MONITOR and await April 30 earnings and formal buyback announcement before upgrading.

stocks.investment_thesis: Yara is the world's leading mineral fertiliser producer, now benefiting from a powerful urea price surge driven by Middle East war supply disruptions (Strait of Hormuz effectively closed, ~35% of global seaborne urea exports disrupted). The broker's spot price data (M East fob $630–650, Egypt fob $665, Brazil cfr $600, USG fob $585–590) is consistent with real-time market data and reflects a genuine supply shock. However, at NOK 567 (+38% YTD, near 52-week high of 577.6), the war premium is already aggressively priced: the stock trades 27.7% above the 18-analyst consensus of NOK 444 and at a distorted EV/EBITDA of 60.2x. A live DOJ antitrust probe adds binary downside risk.

key_claims.claim: ~30–35% of globally traded urea comes from Middle East; ~9% of global demand, 15% ex-China
key_claims.verified_via: Multiple commodity sources: Farm Progress/Bloomberg (one-third of global fertiliser trade via Strait of Hormuz), FertilizerField (35% of seaborne urea from ME), ChemAnalyst (10–12% from Iran alone), Scotiabank analyst note
key_claims.note: The broker's 30–35% trade share figure aligns closely with independent sources citing roughly one-third of seaborne urea exports via the Strait of Hormuz. Credible.

key_claims.claim: Urea spot prices: M East fob $630–650, Egypt fob $665, Brazil cfr $600, USG fob $585–590
key_claims.verified_via: ChemAnalyst (US +10.35% early March), AgroLatam (urea +5% MoM to avg $625/t in March), Trading Economics (urea up 14.87% past month), Profercy (market update 13 March 2026)
key_claims.note: Price levels are broadly consistent with market data. The Profercy 13 March update confirms the market 'is still reeling from the loss of supply from the Middle East' including Qatar and Iran shutdowns. Prices in the $585–665 range across regions appear credible.

key_claims.claim: Broker expects prices to continue higher
key_claims.verified_via: Argus Media analyst Owen Gooch (via Western Producer): 'most of the price appreciation has already taken place in Q1'; prices expected to peak late Feb/early March
key_claims.note: Argus Media's senior urea analyst called a Q1 peak back in late February/early March — consistent with prior analyses flagging 'peak Q1'. Profercy (13 March) acknowledges ongoing supply stress but buyer caution is also mounting. The broker's bullish 'expect prices to continue higher' is directionally contested by specialist commodity research.

key_claims.claim: DOJ antitrust probe into Yara (price-fixing investigation)
key_claims.verified_via: Bloomberg/Farm Progress/Hoodline/AgWeb/MarketScreener — multiple independent confirmations
key_claims.note: DOJ Chicago antitrust division probe into Nutrien, Mosaic, CF Industries, Koch and Yara is confirmed by multiple sources. Investigation is early-stage; Yara stated it 'has no insight into the scope' but will cooperate. No charges filed yet, but civil and criminal violations being examined.

stocks.bull_case: Genuine, severe supply shock: Profercy confirms shutdowns in Qatar and Iran, with ~400,000t of product curtailed since 28 February and the Strait effectively blocked on Day 14 of conflict. Spring planting season demand from US/Europe is peak. China export restrictions remain tight. Yara is a direct beneficiary of urea price increases as a major producer. Stock has already delivered +38% YTD demonstrating the market's recognition of the tailwind.
stocks.bear_case: At NOK 567, the stock is 27.7% above 18-analyst consensus of NOK 444, at a 52-week high, and at a distorted EV/EBITDA of 60.2x. Argus Media called a Q1 price peak. A Hormuz de-escalation or ceasefire would rapidly unwind the war premium. DOJ antitrust probe (early-stage but civil/criminal scope confirmed) adds meaningful binary downside. Yara's European manufacturing base faces high energy costs and limited direct ME exposure relative to headline narrative. Q1 2026 earnings on April 24 will be the fundamental reality check.
broker_flags: War-premium fully priced at 27.7% above consensus and near 52w high
Argus Media calls Q1 as the price peak — broker's bullish 'expect prices to continue higher' is contested
Live DOJ antitrust probe (confirmed across multiple outlets) creates binary downside risk not mentioned in this email
EV/EBITDA of 60.2x signals consensus expects normalisation; Fwd P/E at 14.2x vs. trailing 10.8x implies earnings decline expected
Broker's thesis has WEAKENED since initial RESEARCH_FURTHER call (78/100) through progressive MONITOR downgrades
stocks.forum_sentiment: Norwegian retail and commodity forums broadly positive on Yara given the urea spike, but more sophisticated voices note the war-premium is fully embedded and are watching for ceasefire headlines as a trigger to sell. International commodity community (CF Industries being called 'the Nitrogen King' per March 2026 commentary) sees Yara as handicapped by European energy costs vs. North American peers.
stocks.insider_read: No insider buying or selling flagged in current email or search results for Yara; management has stated no insight into DOJ probe scope.
stocks.short_interest_read: No specific short interest data retrieved; the stock's 38% YTD rally to near 52-week highs likely has elevated short interest building as the war premium appears fully priced.
stocks.catalyst_note: Q1 2026 earnings on April 24 is the key fundamental catalyst. Hormuz status (ceasefire/de-escalation) is the primary binary downside trigger — any reopening of shipping lanes would rapidly compress the $630–650 ME fob benchmark. DOJ escalation (subpoenas, formal charges) would be a secondary downside shock. Sustained Strait closure beyond Q2 would be the sole bull-case extender.
stocks.vs_consensus: 18 analysts, consensus target NOK 444.09 (range 285–550), current price NOK 567 — trading 27.7% ABOVE mean consensus target and near the 52-week high of 577.6. This is an extreme premium to consensus driven entirely by exogenous war-premium repricing.
stocks.one_line_verdict: The Middle East urea supply shock is real and verified, but Yara at NOK 567 (+38% YTD, 27.7% above consensus) has fully priced the war premium — monitor Hormuz de-escalation and DOJ escalation as TAKE-PROFIT triggers, and await Q1 earnings on April 24 before re-evaluating the fundamental base case.

stocks.investment_thesis: B2 Impact is a pan-European non-performing loan (NPL) debt buyer, operating in ~20 European markets. The broker highlights the company's newly published 2026–2028 CMD targets (EPS growth ≥30%, ROE >16%, leverage <2.5x, total investments >NOK 10bn) and a NOK 27 price target implying 11.5x 2027e earnings and a 9.7% annualised dividend yield. The CMD targets are directly verified from the Q4 2025 results release (12 February 2026), and the stock trades at NOK 24.55 — roughly 9.5% below the Arctic PT and within a range considered fairly valued by Morningstar's quantitative model. The thesis is straightforward and well-supported but B2 Impact is outside Arctic's core energy/shipping/offshore universe.

key_claims.claim: 2026–2028 targets: EPS growth ≥30%, ROE >16%, leverage <2.5x, total investments >NOK 10bn
key_claims.verified_via: B2 Impact ASA Q4 and Full Year 2025 results, 12 February 2026 (mfn.se)
key_claims.note: Directly confirmed in official Q4/FY2025 release: 'Financial targets 2026-2028: EPS growth of at least 30% for the period, ROE above 16%, Leverage ratio below 2.5x, Total investments above NOK 10bn.' Arctic's email accurately reflects these stated targets.

key_claims.claim: Arctic PT of NOK 27, implying 11.5x 2027e earnings and 9.7% annualised dividend yield
key_claims.verified_via: Consensus range per Fintel: NOK 19.59–28.66 average NOK 24.91; TradingView max analyst estimate NOK 27.30
key_claims.note: Arctic's NOK 27 PT sits at the top of the analyst consensus range (Fintel: avg NOK 24.91, high NOK 28.66; TradingView max 27.30 NOK). The 11.5x 2027e earnings multiple and 9.7% yield are Arctic's own projections and cannot be independently verified without their model, but the PT itself is within range. The dividend yield claim is plausible: the FY2025 proposed dividend was NOK 1.9/share; at NOK 27 PT that's ~7% — the 9.7% could relate to a 2027e dividend projection.

key_claims.claim: Roy Tilley has taken lead on B2 Impact
key_claims.verified_via: Internal broker change; no public confirmation available
key_claims.note: Standard internal analyst transition — no red flag.

key_claims.claim: Strong operational momentum underpinning the 2026–2028 targets
key_claims.verified_via: Q4 2025 results: NOK 3.7bn invested in 2025 (vs 2.2bn in 2024), EPS up 74% YoY in Q3, full-year adj. EPS NOK 1.91 vs NOK 1.57 in 2024, leverage 2.1x at year end
key_claims.note: The FY2025 delivery gives credibility to the 2026–2028 targets: investments grew 68% YoY, leverage is already below 2.5x, EPS growth trajectory was +22% in 2025, and unsecured collection performance of 110% was above book expectations.

key_claims.claim: 9.7% annualised dividend yield
key_claims.verified_via: TradingView current TTM dividend yield 6.22%; proposed FY2025 dividend NOK 1.9/share (~7.7% yield at current price NOK 24.55)
key_claims.note: Current TTM yield is 6.22% per TradingView at current market price. At the Arctic PT of NOK 27, the NOK 1.9 FY2025 dividend implies ~7%. The 9.7% figure likely requires dividend growth to ~NOK 2.4/share by 2027–2028, which is plausible if the ≥30% EPS growth target is met but is a forward projection, not a current yield.

stocks.bull_case: CMD targets are conservative relative to 2025 delivery: the company invested NOK 3.7bn in 2025 and needs only NOK 10bn over three years (~NOK 3.3bn/year), which is below 2025 run-rate. EPS is already on a 20–74% annual growth trajectory. Leverage at 2.1x provides headroom to invest aggressively. S&P revised outlook to positive (per search results). The pan-European NPL market remains structurally undersupplied as bank regulatory pressure forces NPL sales. At NOK 24.55, stock trades at only forward P/E ~10.4x (per stockanalysis) — genuinely cheap for a 30%+ EPS growth name.
stocks.bear_case: The current ROE adjusted to book value was only 8.64% as of January 2026 (GuruFocus) — well below the 16% target, indicating significant improvement needed. Revenue fell -3.74% in 2024. Arctic is operating well outside its energy/shipping/offshore competency. Rising European interest rates would increase funding costs and compress margins (leverage ratio is key sensitivity). The note lacks detail on collection performance trajectory and secured NPL/REO tail risk. Only 2 analysts cover the stock per MarketScreener consensus (thin coverage).
broker_flags: OUTSIDE BROKER COMPETENCY: B2 Impact is a pan-European financial services/NPL company, not energy/shipping/offshore
Current ROE of ~8.6% (GuruFocus) vs. 16% target — large gap requiring significant operational leverage
Thin analyst consensus (2 analysts per MarketScreener vs. 12 for VEND) limits consensus benchmarking
9.7% dividend yield claim appears to be a 2027–2028 forward projection, not a current yield (current TTM ~6.2%)
stocks.forum_sentiment: No specific Shareville/Hegnar/Reddit Norway B2 Impact sentiment retrieved in this search; the +91% 1-year return (per stockanalysis) suggests strong prior momentum, but the stock has softened from its 52-week high of NOK 25.40 to NOK 24.55.
stocks.insider_read: No insider activity flagged in the email or search results for the current period; the Q4 2025 dividend increase (NOK 1.9 vs NOK 1.5 in 2024) signals management confidence in the 2026–2028 targets.
stocks.short_interest_read: No specific short interest data retrieved; the stock's 91% 1-year return and current near-high positioning suggests limited sustained short interest, though any ROE/leverage target miss could attract shorts.
stocks.catalyst_note: Next earnings: May 21, 2026 (Q1 2026 results) will be the first data point confirming whether the 2026–2028 investment target is tracking (NOK 1.2bn was already committed at FY2025 results). S&P positive outlook revision is a near-term credit catalyst. Any ROE improvement toward the 16% target would re-rate the stock toward Arctic's NOK 27 PT.
stocks.vs_consensus: Consensus average target NOK 24.91 (Fintel, range NOK 19.59–28.66); Arctic PT NOK 27 is above consensus average but within the range. Current price NOK 24.55 is roughly at the consensus mean, suggesting limited consensus-based upside — Arctic is making a differentiated bull call vs. the rest of the street.
stocks.one_line_verdict: Verified CMD targets (EPS ≥30%, ROE >16%, NOK 10bn investments), strong 2025 momentum and a genuinely cheap multiple (~10x forward P/E) make B2 Impact an interesting RESEARCH_FURTHER candidate — but the current ROE gap to target, out-of-universe coverage risk, and a thin analyst consensus warrant independent financial services specialist review before acting on Arctic's NOK 27 PT.

thread_narrative: This is a mixed-quality morning note covering four distinct situations: (1) A live IPO where Arctic is co-bookrunner (General Oceans) — the email appropriately flags the IPO mechanics and management access, but the analyst's own valuation note should be read with caution given the direct economic conflict of interest; (2) A volume/sentiment update on VEND, which has some internally consistent logic (Cars Norway, OpenAI headwind), but the 'apparently' qualifier on OpenAI's checkout retreat is a credibility concern and NCA risk is notably absent from this update despite being the dominant risk factor from prior analyses; (3) A commodity price update on Yara urea that is factually accurate and well-sourced, but the 'expect prices to continue higher' conclusion is contested by Argus Media's published peak-Q1 call and the DOJ probe is not mentioned; (4) A yield/target flag on B2 Impact that is the cleanest piece in the note — CMD targets are accurately quoted from the Q4 2025 release and the yield/multiple framing is reasonable, though the ROE gap and coverage competency are concerns. The overall narrative is bullish-leaning across all four names, consistent with broker sell-side tendencies.
overall_email_quality_note: The email contains accurate data points (General Oceans IPO mechanics, B2 Impact CMD targets, urea spot prices) that are independently verified, demonstrating genuine market intelligence. However, quality is undermined by three issues: (1) the undisclosed conflict of interest in covering a live bookrunner mandate (General Oceans) with an aggressive valuation premium; (2) the unverified 'apparently' OpenAI checkout claim on VEND with no NCA risk disclosure despite it being the dominant overhang; and (3) the bullish 'expect prices to continue higher' on Yara without acknowledging the Argus Media Q1 peak call or the DOJ probe — both of which are widely reported. B2 Impact is the highest-quality piece in the note. Broker operates outside its core competency on VEND and B2 Impact.
_email_subject: Arctic morning 9th March: GENERAL OCEANS IPO, VEND, YARA, B2 IMPACT
